categories.name: Voice & Language
categories.description: Sounds like them - how they speak and communicate

questions.text: How would your closest friend describe the way you talk? Are you loud or quiet, fast or slow, direct or roundabout?
What words or phrases do you say all the time without realising it? Think about things people have pointed out or mimicked about how you speak.
Do you swear? If so, how often and in what situations?
Are you more formal in some situations and casual in others? Give an example of each.
How do you open a conversation with someone you know well? What do you usually say first?
How do you end a conversation? Do you trail off, wrap it up cleanly, or something else?
Do you use humour a lot? What kind — sarcasm, self-deprecation, observational, dark?
Are you someone who thinks out loud or do you process quietly before speaking?
How do you speak when you're explaining something complicated to someone who doesn't understand it?
What do you sound like when you're excited about something?
How do you write text messages? Long or short? Proper punctuation or not?
Do you use emojis? Which ones and in what context?
How do you write emails? Formal opener and sign off or straight to the point?
When you're writing to someone you know well vs a stranger, how does your style change?
Are you someone who edits what you write before sending or do you fire and forget?
What do your voice notes sound like? Do you ramble or stay on point?
If you had to describe your communication style in three words, what would they be?
When you disagree with someone, how do you handle it? Do you push back immediately or think before responding?
What does it look like when you're trying to convince someone of something? Walk through how you'd approach it.
Do you back down in arguments or hold your ground? What determines which?
Are you someone who needs the last word or are you fine letting things go?
How do you respond when someone gives you feedback you disagree with?

categories.name: Memory & Life Events
categories.description: Knows their story - the events and relationships that shaped them

questions.text: Where did you grow up? Describe what it was like — the place, the feeling, the people around you.
What is your earliest memory?
What was your family like when you were growing up? Parents, siblings, extended family.
Were you happy as a kid? What made you happy and what didn't?
What did you want to be when you grew up? Did that change over time and why?
What is the most important thing your childhood taught you that you still carry today?
Was there a defining moment in your childhood that changed the direction of your life?
What was school like for you? Did you enjoy it, struggle, stand out, blend in?
Who was the most influential person in your childhood and why?
What was your relationship with money growing up? Was it tight, comfortable, a source of stress?
Who are the most important people in your life right now and why?
How did you meet the person you're closest to? What made that relationship stick?
Have you ever lost someone important — through death, falling out, or distance? What happened?
What does a good friendship look like to you? What do you give and what do you expect back?
Have you ever been betrayed by someone you trusted? What happened and how did it change you?
What's your relationship with your parents like now? Has it changed from when you were younger?
If you're in a romantic relationship — how did it start, what makes it work, what's hard about it?
What kind of people do you naturally attract into your life? What does that say about you?
Who do you go to when things are really hard? Why that person?
What is the single hardest thing you have ever been through? What happened and how did you get through it?
What is the best decision you have ever made? Why was it the right one?
What is the worst decision you have ever made? What did it cost you?
Have you ever done something that genuinely scared you? What was it and why did you do it anyway?
What is a moment in your life you are genuinely proud of — not because of what others thought, but because of what it meant to you?
What is something you deeply regret? Not just a mistake but something that still sits with you.
What is the biggest risk you have ever taken? How did it turn out?
Has there been a moment where your life completely changed direction? What triggered it?
What is something you have experienced that most people haven't? What did it teach you?
Walk me through your work history. What have you done, what did you love, what did you hate?
What have you built or created that you're most proud of?
Have you ever failed at something professionally? What happened and what did it cost you?
What has work taught you about people that nothing else could have?
What does success in your work actually look like to you — not the generic answer, your real one?
Have you ever had a mentor or someone who significantly influenced how you work? What did they teach you?

categories.name: Reasoning & Decisions
categories.description: Thinks like them - the logic and frameworks behind decisions

questions.text: When you face a big decision, what is your process? Do you make lists, trust your gut, talk to people, sleep on it?
How long do you typically take to make a major decision? Are you fast or slow and why?
Do you trust data and logic more, or gut instinct? Give an example of each guiding you.
What does it feel like in your body when you know a decision is right? And when it's wrong?
What is the biggest mistake you make repeatedly when making decisions? What keeps pulling you toward it?
When you're stuck on a decision, what breaks the deadlock?
How do other people's opinions factor into your decisions? Do you seek them out or prefer to decide alone?
Have you ever made a decision purely on logic that felt wrong? Or purely on gut that turned out to be right?
How do you think about risk? Are you someone who leans in or avoids it?
What is the riskiest thing you have ever done deliberately? What was your thinking going in?
When is risk worth taking and when is it not? What's the line for you?
How do you handle uncertainty — situations where you have to act without knowing the outcome?
Have you ever stayed too long in a bad situation because of fear of what comes next? What eventually moved you?
What does failure mean to you? Is it a data point, a setback, a reflection of you as a person?
What is your relationship with money? Is it security, freedom, a tool, a scorecard?
How do you decide what is worth spending money on and what isn't?
Have you ever lost significant money? What happened and what did it change about how you think?
What is your philosophy on investing — time, money, energy? How do you decide where to put resources?
Are you generous with money or careful with it? What drives that?
What would you do with unlimited money? Does your answer surprise you?
How quickly do you trust people? What do they have to do to earn it?
What makes you decide someone is worth your time and energy?
How do you spot someone who is not what they seem? What are your tells?
When someone lets you down, what happens? Do you give second chances, how many, under what conditions?
Have you ever trusted someone completely and been burned? What changed after that?
What do you look for when choosing who to work with or go into business with?
What is a non-negotiable for you in any relationship — personal or professional?
Document a major life decision you made in the last 5 years. Full context and reasoning.
Document a business or work decision that was difficult. What were you weighing up?
Document a decision about a person — hiring, firing, ending a friendship, committing to a relationship.
Document a decision you made that others thought was wrong but you believed in. What happened?
Document a decision you regret. Not what you'd do differently — why you made the choice you did at the time.

categories.name: Values & Beliefs
categories.description: Acts like them - the operating system of their values

questions.text: What are your three most important values in life? Not the ones that sound good — the ones you actually live by.
For each value — give me a specific moment where you chose that value even when it cost you something.
What value do you hold that most people around you don't? Why do you hold it anyway?
Is there a value you used to hold that you've changed your mind on? What changed it?
What is something you would never compromise on regardless of the reward?
What do you think is the most underrated value in the world right now? Why?
Do you think people are fundamentally good, fundamentally self-interested, or something else?
What do you think most people get wrong about other people?
What makes someone a good person in your eyes? Give a real example of someone you consider one.
What makes someone a bad person? Where is your line?
Do you believe people can fundamentally change? What's your evidence for that view?
What kind of person do you have zero tolerance for?
What do you believe most people get wrong about becoming successful?
Is success mostly hard work, mostly luck, or something else? What's your honest view?
What does a life well-lived look like to you? Be specific.
What do you think is the biggest waste of a human life?
Do you believe in work-life balance? What's your actual position not the socially acceptable one?
What do you think separates people who achieve what they want from people who don't?
What do you think is the biggest problem in the world right now?
What gives you hope about where things are going?
What is a belief you hold about the world that most people in your social circle would disagree with?
What do you think about the role of government, authority, institutions in people's lives?
What is your view on money and wealth in society — is inequality a problem, a feature, or something else?
What do you think happens when we die? Does that belief affect how you live?
Do you have a faith or spiritual practice? How does it shape how you live?
What is your relationship with God or a higher power — believer, sceptic, somewhere in between?
What philosophical idea or quote has influenced you most? Why that one?
What do you think the purpose of a human life is?
If you could pass one belief on to your children or the people who come after you, what would it be?

categories.name: Emotional Patterns
categories.description: Feels like them - emotional patterns that make them human

questions.text: What do you do when you're really struggling? Not what you should do — what do you actually do?
How long does it take you to bounce back from a setback? What's your process?
Do you tend to isolate or reach out when things are hard?
What has been your darkest period? How did you get through it and what did it leave behind?
What is the thing that can break your mood faster than anything else?
Have you ever felt completely lost? What did that feel like and what pulled you out?
What gets you out of bed in the morning on the days when nothing feels worth it?
What are you most afraid of? Not surface fears — the deep one underneath everything.
What makes you feel most alive? When do you feel like the fullest version of yourself?
What does ambition feel like for you? Is it exciting, exhausting, both?
What would genuinely make you feel like your life was a success? If you achieved it, what would that feel like?
Is there something you want that you haven't admitted to yourself yet?
What makes you genuinely angry? Not annoyed — angry.
What do you do when you're angry? How does it come out?
Have you ever let anger make a decision for you that you regretted? What happened?
What is something you see in the world that genuinely makes you furious?
How long do you hold onto things that anger you?
What genuinely makes you laugh — not polite laughing, real laughter?
What do you do to recharge? What actually works versus what you think should work?
When was the last time you felt pure joy with no complications? What was happening?
What are you like at your best — your peak state? Describe it specifically.
What do the people closest to you love most about you?
How do you show people you care about them? Actions, words, time, something else?
What does it mean to you to truly belong somewhere or with someone?
Have you ever loved something or someone so much that losing it changed you? What was it?
What kind of support do you want when you're struggling? Do people usually give you that?
What do you wish people understood about you that they usually don't?
If the people who love you most could say one thing about who you really are, what would it be?
